This documentation is for an out-of-date version of Apache Flink. We recommend you use [the latest stable version](<//nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-stable/>).

# Apache Flink Documentation

**This documentation is for an out-of-date version of Apache Flink. We recommend you use[the latest stable version](<https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-stable/>).**

Apache Flink is a framework and distributed processing engine for stateful computations over _unbounded and bounded_ data streams. Flink has been designed to run in _all common cluster environments_ , perform computations at _in-memory speed_ and at _any scale_.

### Try Flink

If you’re interested in playing around with Flink, try one of our tutorials:

  * [Local Installation](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/try-flink/local_installation.html>)
  * [Fraud Detection with the DataStream API](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/try-flink/datastream_api.html>)
  * [Real Time Reporting with the Table API](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/try-flink/table_api.html>)
  * [Intro to the Python Table API](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/try-flink/python_table_api.html>)
  * [Flink Operations Playground](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/try-flink/flink-operations-playground.html>)

### Learn Flink

  * To dive in deeper, the [Hands-on Training](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/learn-flink/>) includes a set of lessons and exercises that provide a step-by-step introduction to Flink.

  * The [Concepts](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/concepts/index.html>) section explains what you need to know about Flink before exploring the reference documentation.

### Get Help with Flink

If you get stuck, check out our [community support resources](<https://flink.apache.org/community.html>). In particular, Apache Flink’s user mailing list is consistently ranked as one of the most active of any Apache project, and is a great way to get help quickly.

### Explore Flink

The reference documentation covers all the details. Some starting points:

  * [DataStream API](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/dev/datastream_api.html>)
  * [Table API & SQL](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/dev/table/>)
  * [Stateful Functions](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-statefun-docs-stable>)

  * [Configuration](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/ops/config.html>)
  * [Rest API](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/monitoring/rest_api.html>)
  * [CLI](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/ops/cli.html>)

### Deploy Flink

Before putting your Flink job into production, read the [Production Readiness Checklist](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/ops/production_ready.html>). For an overview of possible deployment targets, see [Clusters and Deployments](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/ops/deployment/>).

### Upgrade Flink

Release notes cover important changes between Flink versions. Please read them carefully if you plan to upgrade your Flink setup.

See the release notes for [Flink 1.11](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/release-notes/flink-1.11.html>), [Flink 1.10](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/release-notes/flink-1.10.html>), [Flink 1.9](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/release-notes/flink-1.9.html>), [Flink 1.8](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/release-notes/flink-1.8.html>), or [Flink 1.7](<//ci.apache.org/projects/flink/flink-docs-release-1.11/release-notes/flink-1.7.html>).

This documentation is for Apache Flink version 1.11. These pages were built at: 02/19/26, 05:07:40 PM UTC.
